text: Hello. This is 6 Minute English from BBC Learning English. I'm Neil. And I'm Bekka.
zh: 大家好,欢迎收听BBC英语学习之《六分钟英语》。我是Neil。我是Bekka。

text: Neil, I noticed that you were checking your phone before you came into the studio to record this programme. Yes, I probably was.
zh: Neil,我注意到你在进来演播室录这期节目之前一直在看手机。是的,大概是吧。

text: You know, Bekka, I spend far too much time looking at my phone and I really don't like it.
zh: 你知道吗,Bekka,我花在看手机上的时间实在太多了,我真的不喜欢这样。

text: How about you? I don't think you're alone. I also spend way too much time looking at my phone.
zh: 你呢?我觉得你不是一个人。我也花了太多时间盯着手机看。

text: Well, Bekka, we are both adults, but today we're talking about screen time and
zh: 嗯,Bekka,我们俩都是成年人,不过今天我们要聊的是屏幕使用时间和

text: children. Screen time – that's how much time you spend using devices like smartphones, tablets and laptops.
zh: 孩子的问题。屏幕使用时间——指的是你花多少时间使用智能手机、平板电脑和笔记本电脑之类的设备。

text: Yes, and it's a particular worry of many parents that their children spend too much time on these devices.
zh: 是的,很多家长都特别担心自己的孩子在这些设备上花太多时间。

text: At the end of last year, Australia banned access to social media apps for under
zh: 去年年底,澳大利亚禁止16岁以下

text: -16s, and countries like the UK are considering similar measures. So, why is this?
zh: 青少年使用社交媒体应用,英国等国也在考虑采取类似措施。那么,这是为什么呢?

text: In this episode, we'll hear from a clinical psychologist and research associate at the University of Cambridge talking about screen time and children.
zh: 在这一期节目中,我们将听到剑桥大学的一位临床心理学家兼研究员谈论屏幕使用时间与儿童的话题。

text: As usual, we'll be learning some useful new words and phrases, and remember,
zh: 像往常一样,我们会学习一些实用的新单词和短语,别忘了,

text: you'll find all this episode's vocabulary along with the transcript on our website,
zh: 本期节目的所有词汇和文字稿都可以在我们的网站上找到,

text: bbclearningenglish .com. But first, Neil, I have a question for you.
zh: bbclearningenglish.com。不过首先,Neil,我有个问题要问你。

text: According to the UK media regulator, how many children aged 3 to 4 have a smartphone?
zh: 根据英国媒体监管机构的数据,3到4岁的孩子中有多少人拥有智能手机?

text: Is it a 1 in 20, b 1 in 10, or c 1 in 5?
zh: 是a. 二十分之一,b. 十分之一,还是c. 五分之一?

text: Wow! Well, I'm going to guess 1 in 20, but I think it's probably more than that.
zh: 哇!我猜是二十分之一,不过我觉得实际比例可能更高。

text: Well, we'll find out the answer later. Dr Emily Gudeka is a research associate at the University of Cambridge.
zh: 好,我们稍后揭晓答案。Emily Gudeka博士是剑桥大学的一位研究员。

text: Here she tells Shona McCullum, presenter of BBC World Service programme TechLife,
zh: 她在BBC世界服务频道节目《TechLife》中,对主持人Shona McCullum

text: her thoughts on whether we should take away children's screen devices completely.
zh: 谈了自己对于是否应该完全禁止孩子使用屏幕设备的看法。

text: Yeah, I think it's not as much as getting them away from technology,
zh: 是的,我认为重点不在于让孩子完全远离科技,

text: but being really intentional about how we use technology with children.
zh: 而在于我们如何有意识地、有目的地和孩子一起使用科技产品。

text: For me, the bar absolutely needs to be higher.
zh: 对我来说,这个标准绝对需要提高。

text: And therefore, I think about how to design
zh: 因此,我会思考如何设计

text: products that think about childhood first and really think about how children
zh: 真正以童年为本的产品,认真考虑孩子

text: at different stages learn and develop and then see if there's a way that technology can enable that.
zh: 在不同阶段是如何学习和成长的,然后看看科技是否有办法促成这一点。

text: Emily highlights how rather than stopping children from using technology,
zh: Emily强调,与其阻止孩子使用科技产品,

text: parents need to be more intentional about how their children use it.
zh: 家长更需要有意识地、有计划地引导孩子如何使用它。

text: An intention is a plan or reason to do something.
zh: intention(意图)指的是做某事的计划或理由。

text: So to be intentional is to act with that plan or reason in mind.
zh: 所以intentional(有意为之的)就是带着这种计划或理由去行动。

text: It may make you act more carefully as a result. Emily also says the bar needs to be higher.
zh: 这可能会让你的行事更加谨慎。Emily还提到,标准需要提高。

text: The idiom the bar is low means that the standards, expectations or requirements are minimal and
zh: 习语the bar is low(标准很低)意思是标准、期望或要求非常低,

text: very easy to meet. Therefore, she believes Emily believes the bar needs to be higher. Parents need to make their expectations higher.
zh: 很容易达到。因此,Emily认为标准需要提高。家长需要提高自己的期望。

text: They need to think or act more carefully when considering how much time children spend with technology.
zh: 在考虑孩子使用科技产品的时间时,他们需要更谨慎地思考和行动。

text: You might also hear people say, set the bar higher or lower.
zh: 你也可能听到人们说,把标准set the bar higher或lower,意思是提高或降低标准。

text: Emily believes that being more intentional and setting the bar higher will enable children to learn and develop alongside
zh: Emily认为,更有意识地行动并提高标准,将有助于孩子在使用科技的同时,

text: technology. If you enable something, you encourage somebody's ability to do something or to make something possible.
zh: 学习和成长。如果你enable(促成)某件事,就是在鼓励某人做某事的能力,或者使某件事成为可能。

text: Now let's hear Dr Becky Kennedy, a clinical psychologist and founder of an online parenting platform
zh: 现在我们来听听Becky Kennedy博士的看法,她是一位临床心理学家,也是一个在线育儿平台的创始人,

text: talking about how parents feel about tech and children for BBC World Service programme Tech Life.
zh: 她在BBC世界服务频道节目《Tech Life》中谈到了家长对科技与孩子这一话题的看法。

text: I find parents are very, very eager to understand what's really happening around technology in kids and
zh: 我发现家长们都非常、非常渴望了解孩子身边的科技到底是怎么回事,

text: then have practical, realistic things they can do in their home to make little shifts that feel better.
zh: 然后希望能有一些切实可行的方法,可以在家里做出一些小小的改变,让情况变得更好。

text: Becky has found that many parents are eager to understand their children's use of technology.
zh: Becky发现,许多家长都渴望了解孩子使用科技产品的情况。

text: Eager is an adjective that describes wanting to do or have something very much.
zh: Eager(渴望的)是一个形容词,形容非常想要做某事或拥有某物。

text: Becky also talks about making little shifts.
zh: Becky还提到了making little shifts(做出一些小小的改变)。

text: A shift is a small change. It can be used as a verb or a noun.
zh: Shift(转变)指的是一个小的变化。它既可以用作动词,也可以用作名词。

text: Becky shares an example of a shift that can help parents reduce children's screen time, moving the device somewhere it can't be seen.
zh: Becky举了一个例子,说明一个小小的改变可以帮助家长减少孩子的屏幕时间,那就是把设备放到看不见的地方。

text: You can even say to a kid, you know why I moved it? It's actually because
zh: 你甚至可以告诉孩子:你知道我为什么把它挪走吗?其实是因为

text: it's a really hard thing for me to expect from you, to see a screen in a room and not want to use it. I wasn't setting you up for success.
zh: 让你看到一个屏幕却不去用它,这对我来说是很难做到的事情,我不该这样为难你。我这样做并没有让你有成功的条件。

text: Becky explains how it's difficult for children to be expected to reduce their screen time if they can see their device.
zh: Becky解释说,如果孩子能看到设备,却被要求减少使用时间,这对他们来说是很困难的。

text: Having the device in view doesn't set them up for
zh: 让设备一直在眼前出现,并不能为他们创造

text: success. If you set someone up for something, you prepare them for it.
zh: 成功的条件。如果你set someone up for something(为某人创造条件),就是在为他们做准备。

text: Yes. For example, earlier I set Neil up for a shock when I asked him a question about screen time.
zh: 是的。比如说,刚才我问Neil关于屏幕时间的问题时,就让他大吃一惊。

text: Neil, I asked you, according to the UK media regulator, how many children aged 3 to 4 have a smartphone?
zh: Neil,我问你,根据英国媒体监管机构的数据,3到4岁的孩子中有多少人拥有智能手机?

text: And I said 1 in 20. Well, it is in fact, see, 1 in 5.
zh: 我猜的是二十分之一。而实际上,答案是五分之一。

text: Wow, I thought it might be more than I said. Now let's recap the vocabulary we've learned,
zh: 哇,我猜想应该比我说的比例更高些。现在让我们来回顾一下今天学到的词汇,

text: starting with intentional. It's an adjective that describes acting with a plan or reason in mind.
zh: 首先是intentional。这是一个形容词,形容带着计划或理由去行动。

text: We had the expression, the bar needs to be higher.
zh: 我们还学了一个表达,the bar needs to be higher(标准需要提高)。

text: To set the bar higher is to make your expectations higher.
zh: Set the bar higher就是提高你的期望标准。

text: We also had enable. This is to encourage someone's ability to do something or to make something possible.
zh: 我们还学了enable,意思是鼓励某人做某事的能力,或使某事成为可能。

text: To be eager is to want to do or have something very much.
zh: Eager(渴望的)指的是非常想做某事或拥有某物。

text: A shift is a small change. You can also use it as a verb to mean changing something slightly.
zh: Shift(转变)指的是一个小的变化,也可以用作动词,表示对某事做出小小的调整。

text: And if you set someone up for something, you prepare them for it.
zh: 如果你set someone up for something,就是在为某人做好准备。

text: Once again, our six minutes are up. If you enjoyed this episode, you'll find a quiz and
zh: 我们的六分钟时间又到了。如果你喜欢这一期节目,可以在我们的网站bbclearningenglish.com上找到测验题,

text: a free worksheet to practice the vocabulary you've learnt on our website, bbclearningenglish .com.
zh: 以及免费的练习册,帮助你巩固所学的词汇。

text: See you again soon, but for now, it's Goodbye.
zh: 我们下次再见,现在,先说再见。
